page: Microsoft Azure D4_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the Dv5 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with Hyper-Threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities but weaker multi-core scaling. The stress-ng single-core CPU test scores 1897.28, placing it in the average tier, whereas the multi-core CPU score of 3869.85 is relatively weak. Similarly, Geekbench scores show an average single-core performance of 1653.0 and a weaker multi-core performance of 3526.0. Memory bandwidth tests reveal a weak read-only small block cached speed of 208.57 GB/sec and a weak uncached large block read speed of 29.82 GB/sec, though uncached large block write speed is average at 25.45 GB/sec.

In terms of workload execution, the instance performs moderately in text processing and encryption tasks, with a Passmark CPU encryption score of 3643.26 and a static web serving throughput of 6.6 GB/sec. However, it exhibits weaker performance in database operations, scoring 2045.71 on Passmark database tests, and Redis SET operations at 3.98 million operations per second. Small-scale language model inference is viable, achieving 500.6 tokens per second for prompt processing and 141.13 tokens per second for text generation using a 135M model. This hardware profile is best suited for general-purpose workloads such as light web serving, basic application hosting, and low-intensity background processing.
description: Microsoft Azure D4_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance from the Dv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C x86_64 processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores with Hyper-Threading, a 2.8 GHz clock speed, and 16.0 GB of RAM. The instance does not include local storage or a GPU. Benchmark data shows average single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1897.28 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1653.0, but weaker multi-core scaling and memory read bandwidth. It delivers average performance in static web serving at 6.6 GB/sec and small-scale LLM inference, while showing weaker results in database operations and Redis SET tasks. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for standard general-purpose workloads, including light web hosting, basic application servers, and moderate text processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB of RAM, and no local storage, suitable for light web serving and basic application hosting.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering balanced entry-level performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and a standard memory-to-core ratio for everyday application workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM
No local storage or integrated GPU accelerators included
Average single-core CPU performance and static web serving throughput
Weaker multi-core scaling and database operation performance
Suitable for general-purpose application hosting, light web serving, and basic text processing
